# ICJIA File Accessibility Audit

> Automated PDF accessibility scoring tool built by Illinois Criminal Justice Information Authority.

This web application analyzes PDF documents for accessibility compliance and produces a detailed audit report scored against WCAG 2.1 Level AA and ADA Title II requirements.

## What it does

- Accepts a PDF file upload (up to 50 MB)
- Analyzes the document across up to 11 accessibility categories (nine in Strict mode; Practical mode adds PDF/UA Compliance Signals and reserves Color Contrast)
- Returns two score profiles side-by-side — Strict (semantics-first for ADA/WCAG/ITTAA publication review) and Practical (broader remediation/progress view) — each with an overall score (0-100), letter grade (A-F), and per-category findings
- Provides remediation guidance with WCAG success criteria references
- Exports reports in Word (.docx), HTML, Markdown, and JSON formats
- JSON export includes machine-readable WCAG mappings, prioritized remediation plans, and LLM-ready context for both profiles

## Scoring modes

The app always computes two profiles against the same document:

- Strict semantic score — primary view for Illinois agency publication / ADA / WCAG 2.1 / ITTAA-oriented review. Emphasizes programmatically determinable headings, table semantics, and logical reading order.
- Practical readiness score — secondary progress view more closely aligned to broader weighted remediation workflows, including a dedicated PDF/UA Compliance Signals category covering tagging, MarkInfo, tab order, PDF/UA identifiers, and list/table legality. Not a compliance claim.

Illinois IITAA 2.1 references PDF/UA (§504.2.2) for authoring-tool export capability, while framing final non-web document accessibility through WCAG 2.1 (§E205.4) — which is why Strict is the primary publication lens and Practical layers in PDF/UA-oriented signals.

## Scoring categories (Strict / Practical weights)

1. Text Extractability — 20% / 17.5% — WCAG 1.3.1, 1.4.5
2. Document Title & Language — 15% / 13% — WCAG 2.4.2, 3.1.1
3. Heading Structure — 15% / 13% — WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6
4. Alt Text on Images — 15% / 13% — WCAG 1.1.1
5. PDF/UA Compliance Signals — Practical only / 9.5% — WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.1
6. Bookmarks / Navigation — 10% / 8.5% — WCAG 2.4.5
7. Table Markup — 10% / 8.5% — WCAG 1.3.1
8. Color Contrast — N/A / N/A (4.5% reserved) — WCAG 1.4.3
9. Link & URL Quality — 5% / 4.5% — WCAG 2.4.4
10. Reading Order — 5% / 4% — WCAG 1.3.2
11. Form Accessibility — 5% / 4% — WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2

## Grade scale

- A: 90-100 (Excellent)
- B: 80-89 (Good)
- C: 70-79 (Needs Improvement)
- D: 60-69 (Poor)
- F: 0-59 (Failing)

## Standards alignment

- WCAG 2.1 Level AA
- ADA Title II (effective April 2026)
- Section 508
- PDF/UA (ISO 14289-1)

## API

The application exposes a REST API:

- POST /api/analyze - Upload a PDF for analysis (multipart/form-data, field: "file")
- POST /api/reports - Create a shareable report link (JSON body: { report })
- GET /api/reports/:id - Retrieve a shared report by ID
- GET /api/health - Health check endpoint

## Links

- Website: https://audit.icjia.app
- Source: https://github.com/ICJIA/file-accessibility-audit
- FAQs: https://accessibility.icjia.app
- Organization: https://icjia.illinois.gov
